label: Security Footguns
summary: Unsafe defaults and security-sensitive misuse patterns.

rules.label: Rust From Utf8 Unchecked Boundary
rules.description: Unchecked UTF-8 conversion at a repository or service boundary.
rules.explanation: In Rust, Rust From Utf8 Unchecked Boundary matters because the pattern here, Unchecked UTF-8 conversion at a repository or service boundary, lets layer-specific contracts leak into places that should stay simpler and easier to test.
rules.fix: For Rust From Utf8 Unchecked Boundary in Rust, choose one layer to own the contract shape and perform the mapping there instead of passing boundary-specific types through the stack.

rules.label: Rust Rc Cycle Parent Link
rules.description: Rc-based parent/back-reference shapes that likely need Weak on the reverse edge.
rules.explanation: In Rust, Rust Rc Cycle Parent Link matters because the pattern here, Rc-based parent/back-reference shapes that likely need Weak on the reverse edge, is worth review because a small convenience shortcut can erase the guarantees people assume Rust code is giving them.
rules.fix: For Rust Rc Cycle Parent Link in Rust, prefer safe-by-default APIs, validate boundary input, and isolate sensitive behavior behind strongly typed helpers.

rules.label: Rust Release Profile Missing Overflow Checks

rules.label: Rust Release Profile Panic Unwind

rules.label: Rust Split at Unchecked External Input
rules.description: Slice splitting and range indexing on externally-derived offsets without obvious bounds guards.
rules.explanation: In Rust, Rust Split at Unchecked External Input matters because the pattern here, Slice splitting and range indexing on externally-derived offsets without obvious bounds guards, removes a guardrail or signal that reviewers expect before trusting the surrounding code.
rules.fix: For Rust Split at Unchecked External Input in Rust, add obvious bounds guards or move the code to a path where that safeguard is guaranteed before the risky work happens.

rules.description: Raw std::thread::spawn blocks that call async work without an explicit runtime handoff.
